justification: All 6 pass criteria met: (1) All 5 valid transitions from AC1 implemented via VALID_TRANSITIONS map, (2) InvalidTransitionError extends Error with code: 'INVALID_TRANSITION' property, (3) Both DELIVERED and CANCELLED have empty Sets enforcing terminal state behavior, (4) Tests cover all 5 valid transitions plus 10+ invalid transitions (well exceeding the 2 minimum), (5) AC6 SHIPPED->PENDING explicitly tested, (6) No external libraries — pure TypeScript with ts-jest only in devDependencies, (7) acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs with status 'implemented'.

observations.text: Exhaustive terminal state testing — tests every possible target from both CANCELLED and DELIVERED, not just a sample
53 tests across 2 suites is thorough; uses test.each for systematic coverage
InvalidTransitionError subclass is a superset of the spec requirement (Error with code property) — correctly documented as a deviation
O(1) Set-based transition lookup is clean and idiomatic
Duplicate implementation in both .tmp/ and .tmp/order-state-machine/ is unnecessary — creates maintenance surface for no clear benefit
